sanskrit:
कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन्।
इन्द्रियार्थान् विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचारः स उच्यते॥
transliteration:
karmendriyāṇi saṃyamya ya āste manasā smaran |
indriyārthān vimūḍhātmā mithyācāraḥ sa ucyate ||
literal_meaning: Having restrained the organs of action, one who sits remembering sense-objects with the mind is called a deluded soul and a hypocrite.
contextual_meaning: External restraint without inner alignment creates cognitive dissonance; the mind’s covert craving undermines the ego’s claim of renunciation, producing ‘false self’ behavior.
psychological_state: Performative asceticism masking unmet desire; a split between idealized spiritual identity and repressed sensory longing.
trigger_scenario: Posting minimalist lifestyle photos on social media while obsessively online-shopping in private.
anchor_text: True renunciation is when mind and senses let go together.
